content: I want to call methods defined in kernel/locking/rtmutex_common.h from Rust code. Since it's not in the include/linux/ directory, it doesn't seem like I can include it in a helper. How should I approach this?
(Specifically, I want to use the proxy APIs currently only used by futexes.)

Temporarily something like below?:

diff --git a/rust/helpers.c b/rust/helpers.c
index 9ac4a06e6bbe..ddb6ce04b3df 100644
--- a/rust/helpers.c
+++ b/rust/helpers.c
@@ -25,6 +25,7 @@
 #define CREATE_TRACE_POINTS
 #include <trace/events/rust.h>

+#include "../kernel/locking/rtmutex_common.h"
 __noreturn void rust_helper_BUG(void)
 {
        BUG();
@@ -57,6 +58,12 @@ void rust_helper_trace_arc_drop_inner(const char *name,
 }
 EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(rust_helper_trace_arc_drop_inner);

+void rust_helper_xxx(void)
+{
+       rt_mutex_init_waiter(NULL);
+}
+EXPORT_SYMBOL_GPL(rust_helper_xxx);
+
 /*
  * We use `bindgen`'s `--size_t-is-usize` option to bind the C `size_t` type
  * as the Rust `usize` type, so we can use it in contexts where Rust
Yeah, for the moment I would do that. Note that for private APIs, we will need to be very clear to upstream maintainers that we will use them on Rust's side.

Eventually, when the kernel crate is split, we will want to limit the access to such private C APIs to particular crates, i.e. it would really be a case where the Rust code is considered part of the same privacy boundary as the C side, even if written in a different language.
